message:
fix(fcp-ratelimit): harden exponential backoff against NaN, infinity, and zero-duration edge cases

Guard next_backoff() against several floating-point edge cases that
could cause panics or nonsensical delays:

- Return max immediately if initial or max duration is zero
- Clamp base to max when powi() produces infinity (extreme multiplier
  or attempt values)
- Clamp jitter factor to [0.0, 1.0] range to prevent NaN propagation
  from out-of-range jitter configuration
- Final guard before Duration::from_secs_f64() ensures the result is
  finite and non-negative, falling back to max on any invalid value

This prevents Duration::from_secs_f64 panics that would crash the
connector runtime on pathological retry configurations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
